title: Listening - Part 3: Organising a Family Celebration
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose a location for a family celebration. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the location. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose a location for a family celebration. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the location. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about choosing a place for a family celebration.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My parents planned an outdoor party for their anniversary in our garden, because it has plenty of room and would cost nothing. As the date approached, the forecast showed heavy rain all weekend. [19]We moved the celebration to the village hall because we needed somewhere covered where everyone could eat and dance without the event depending on the weather.[/19] The rain was as bad as predicted, so although the hall needed decorating quickly, nobody regretted the decision.||Speaker 2 Man: For my grandfather’s ninetieth birthday, several relatives suggested a stylish restaurant upstairs in an old building. The menu was excellent, but there was no lift, and my grandfather now uses a walking frame. [20]We chose a hotel dining room instead because he could enter directly from the car park and reach his seat without facing steps or long corridors.[/20] It was important that the person whose birthday we were celebrating felt comfortable, rather than exhausted before the meal started.||Speaker 3 Woman: My sister's birthday gathering included cousins ranging from toddlers to teenagers. A quiet café might have suited the adults, but we knew the younger ones would become restless during a long meal. [21]We booked a room at a country park because it also had play areas, a small animal farm and trails where the children could run around safely.[/21] They hardly looked at their phones all afternoon, while the adults had time to talk. That made it the right choice.||Speaker 4 Man: We wanted to celebrate my parents renewing their wedding vows, but there are a lot of us in the family and hiring a restaurant for everyone was beyond what we could afford. [22]The community centre allowed us to rent a large room cheaply and bring in our own food, so we could invite everyone without spending more than we had planned.[/22] It wasn't luxurious, but relatives helped decorate it and the home-made buffet was far better than any formal meal.||Speaker 5 Woman: My brother was returning from working abroad and told us only four days beforehand that he would be home on his birthday. We wanted to arrange a surprise gathering, but restaurants were full because it was a holiday weekend. [23]A small sports club had a function room free after a cancellation and could take our booking immediately, which mattered more than its appearance or location.[/23] We borrowed decorations, ordered food quickly and somehow kept everything secret until he walked through the door.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My parents planned an outdoor party for their anniversary in our garden, because it has plenty of room and would cost nothing. As the date approached, the forecast showed heavy rain all weekend. We moved the celebration to the village hall because we needed somewhere covered where everyone could eat and dance without the event depending on the weather. The rain was as bad as predicted, so although the hall needed decorating quickly, nobody regretted the decision.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: For my grandfather’s ninetieth birthday, several relatives suggested a stylish restaurant upstairs in an old building. The menu was excellent, but there was no lift, and my grandfather now uses a walking frame. We chose a hotel dining room instead because he could enter directly from the car park and reach his seat without facing steps or long corridors. It was important that the person whose birthday we were celebrating felt comfortable, rather than exhausted before the meal started.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My sister's birthday gathering included cousins ranging from toddlers to teenagers. A quiet café might have suited the adults, but we knew the younger ones would become restless during a long meal. We booked a room at a country park because it also had play areas, a small animal farm and trails where the children could run around safely. They hardly looked at their phones all afternoon, while the adults had time to talk. That made it the right choice.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: We wanted to celebrate my parents renewing their wedding vows, but there are a lot of us in the family and hiring a restaurant for everyone was beyond what we could afford. The community centre allowed us to rent a large room cheaply and bring in our own food, so we could invite everyone without spending more than we had planned. It wasn't luxurious, but relatives helped decorate it and the home-made buffet was far better than any formal meal.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My brother was returning from working abroad and told us only four days beforehand that he would be home on his birthday. We wanted to arrange a surprise gathering, but restaurants were full because it was a holiday weekend. A small sports club had a function room free after a cancellation and could take our booking immediately, which mattered more than its appearance or location. We borrowed decorations, ordered food quickly and somehow kept everything secret until he walked through the door.

options.text: It provided protection from bad weather.
It was easy for an older guest to use.
It gave the group complete privacy.
It kept costs within their budget.
It was available at very short notice.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The hall was selected so the anniversary party could proceed despite forecast rain (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The family selected an accessible venue that would be comfortable for the elderly birthday guest (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The country park offered varied safe activities for the children attending the celebration (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The community centre made it possible to hold a large celebration at an affordable price (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The family booked the function room because it was available when they had very little time to organise (evidence [23]).
